topic: A person learns their childhood bully became a therapist
raw_output:
[story 1]

The receptionist’s smile was stretched thin, a performance that didn't quite reach her eyes. I shifted my weight on the uncomfortable visitor’s chair, the cheap vinyl squeaking under my movement. "Mr. Thorne is just finishing up," she chirped, her voice a little too bright. I crossed my arms tightly over my chest, the fabric pulling across my shoulders. I stared at the abstract painting on the wall, willing the silence to stretch out, hoping it would somehow swallow the lingering tension in the room. When the door finally opened, he walked in. It was him. Same broad shoulders, same slightly arrogant tilt to his chin, but now he wore a tailored charcoal suit and an expression that was unnervingly calm. He offered a polite, measured nod. "Hello," he said, his voice deep and modulated, utterly devoid of the sharp mockery I remembered. I felt a distinct tightening in my jaw, a physical clenching that I fought to suppress.

[story 2]

I tapped my foot against the polished linoleum floor, a restless, repetitive rhythm. Every time he moved within the room, I cataloged it—the precise placement of his hands, the way he leaned against the mahogany desk. It was irritating, this sudden proximity to the source of so much past aggravation. When he finally turned to face me, his gaze was steady, analytical, and utterly lacking in any trace of the childhood tormentor. "Come in, Alex," he greeted me, his tone professional, almost gentle. I forced a slight, dismissive shrug, my eyes flicking over his expensive watch. I wanted to lean back, to create distance, but my body remained stiff, rooted to the spot. A low, almost inaudible sigh escaped me, a puff of air that seemed to betray the considerable internal friction I was experiencing.

[story 3]

"So, this is the session," he began, his voice smooth as aged whiskey. He sat across from me, radiating an aura of effortless control that made my own agitation feel clumsy and juvenile. I kept my hands folded on my lap, gripping the edges of my trousers as if they were anchors in choppy water. I watched him, searching for the familiar spark of malice, the slight sneer that used to preface his worst remarks. It wasn't there. Instead, there was a disconcerting empathy in his eyes, a deep, unsettling understanding. I cleared my throat, the sound rough in the quiet room. "Right," I managed, my tone deliberately flat, trying to inject a coolness into my voice that felt entirely manufactured. I could feel the heat rising in my neck, a prickling sensation beneath my skin that demanded I find an exit, any exit.

"So, this is the session," he began, his voice smooth as aged whiskey. He sat across from me, radiating an aura of effortless control that made my own agitation feel clumsy and juvenile. I kept my hands folded on my lap, gripping the edges of my trousers as if they were anchors in choppy water. "Right," I managed, my tone deliberately flat, trying to inject a coolness into my voice that felt entirely manufactured.
